specific_mechanism: Varves are paired light/dark seasonal laminae deposited in glacial proglacial or anoxic-bottom lakes. Each year produces one couplet (summer organic-rich / winter clastic). Counting varves backward gives an absolute chronology at annual resolution. Biogenic lamina thickness encodes growing-season climate, and varve composition records solar forcing, hydrology, and depositional regime changes over centuries-to-millennia.
llm_application: Treat per-training-step gradient checkpoints (or model-state snapshots at fixed intervals) as varves: each step deposits a 'lamina' in the parameter-delta record. Composition of each lamina (gradient direction, magnitude, noise level, layer-wise rank, attention pattern entropy) is a multi-proxy 'biogenic' record of the training regime. Phase-coherence: the periodicity of LR-schedule cycles maps to seasonal cycles, and proxy reconstruction from the lamina stack can reverse-engineer the training data distribution or curriculum order from the parameter-delta archive alone.
novelty_claim: Checkpoint/restore strategies (2512.24511, GoCkpt 2511.07035) and longitudinal trajectory reconstruction (medRxiv 2026.02.10) study LLM checkpoints for engineering or for clinical-trajectory reconstruction. None proposes the parameter-delta history as a multi-proxy paleo-archive readable for *backward* reconstruction of training-data composition. Open question whether 'training varve' archeology is substantively new or implicit in influence-function literature.
